description: Disturb Opponents' No Trump with 2-level overcalls showing specific suit patterns
purpose: Compete against an opponent's 1NT opening by describing your distribution cheaply, letting partner judge fit and level

teaching.tradeoff: DONT overcalls commit to the 2-level immediately — if partner has a misfit, you may go down.
teaching.principle: Against a strong 1NT, disrupting their communication is more valuable than finding your own perfect contract.
commonMistakes: Double shows a single long suit (not penalty) — advancer must relay to 2C to discover which suit
2H shows hearts AND a higher suit (spades) — it's not a single-suited overcall

explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Does not hold 5-4 or better in both majors
Does not hold 5+ diamonds with a 4+ card major

explanationEntries.displayText: 5+ clubs with a 4+ card higher suit
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Does not hold 5+ clubs with a 4+ card higher suit
Does not hold 6+ spades

explanationEntries.displayText: 6+ cards in one suit (not spades), no secondary 4+ suit
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Does not hold a single-suited hand (non-spades) without a secondary suit
The single suit is not clubs
The single suit is not diamonds
The single suit is not hearts

explanationEntries.displayText: With clubs as anchor, the higher suit is diamonds
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: The higher suit is not diamonds

explanationEntries.displayText: With clubs as anchor, the higher suit is hearts
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: The higher suit is not hearts

explanationEntries.displayText: With clubs as anchor, the higher suit is spades
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: The higher suit is not spades

explanationEntries.displayText: With diamonds as anchor, the major is hearts
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: The major is not hearts

explanationEntries.displayText: With diamonds as anchor, the major is spades
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: The major is not spades
Fewer than 3 hearts to support partner
Fewer than 3 spades to support partner
Fewer than 3 diamonds to support partner
Fewer than 3 clubs to support partner

explanationEntries.displayText: 6+ in clubs or diamonds (long minor for escape)
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Does not hold 6+ in either minor
Longer minor is not clubs
Longer minor is not diamonds
Fewer than 6 diamonds
Fewer than 6 hearts
Fewer than 6 spades
Fewer than 6 clubs

explanationEntries.displayText: Double: One long suit (6+), relay for identity
Pass: No DONT action (hand does not qualify)
2S: Prefer spades (3+ spades, fewer than 3 hearts)
3C: Escape to long clubs (6+), no major fit
3D: Escape to long diamonds (6+), no major fit
Pass: Accept diamonds as trump (3+ diamonds)
2H: Relay asking which major partner holds
Pass: Accept clubs as trump (3+ clubs)
2D: Relay asking which higher suit partner holds
Pass: Accept spades as trump (3+ spades)
Pass: Accept spades by default (no better option)
Pass: Higher suit is diamonds (already bid via relay)
Pass: The major is hearts (already bid via relay)

teachingLabel.name: Opponent's 1NT
teachingLabel.summary: Opponent opens 1NT, triggering the DONT convention

teachingLabel.name: 2H — both majors
teachingLabel.summary: Show both majors (5-4 or better either way) over opponent's 1NT

teachingLabel.name: 2D — diamonds + a major
teachingLabel.summary: Show diamonds plus an unspecified major over opponent's 1NT

teachingLabel.name: 2C — clubs + higher suit
teachingLabel.summary: Show clubs plus an unspecified higher suit over opponent's 1NT

teachingLabel.name: 2S — natural spades
teachingLabel.summary: Overcall naturally with a 6+ card spade suit over opponent's 1NT

teachingLabel.name: X — single suited (not spades)
teachingLabel.summary: Show a single long suit (6+, not spades) — advancer must relay 2C to discover which

teachingLabel.name: Pass (no DONT bid)
teachingLabel.summary: Decline to compete — hand does not meet DONT overcall requirements

teachingLabel.name: Pass — accept hearts
teachingLabel.summary: Accept hearts as trumps with 3+ card support for overcaller's major

teachingLabel.name: 2S — prefer spades
teachingLabel.summary: Correct to spades when lacking heart support after overcaller's both-majors bid

clauses.description: Has a long minor is clubs (longer minor)
No has spade support (prefer major fit when 3+ spades)

teachingLabel.name: 3C — minor escape
teachingLabel.summary: Escape to clubs with a long suit when lacking support for either major

clauses.description: Has a long minor is diamonds (longer minor)
No has spade support (prefer major fit when 3+ spades)

teachingLabel.name: 3D — minor escape
teachingLabel.summary: Escape to diamonds with a long suit when lacking support for either major

teachingLabel.name: Pass — accept diamonds
teachingLabel.summary: Accept diamonds as trumps with 3+ card support for overcaller's minor

teachingLabel.name: 2S — bypass (6+ spades)
teachingLabel.summary: Bypass the relay with 6+ spades, placing the contract directly

teachingLabel.name: 2H — relay (ask for major)
teachingLabel.summary: Ask overcaller to reveal which major they hold alongside diamonds

teachingLabel.name: Pass — accept clubs
teachingLabel.summary: Accept clubs as trumps with 3+ card support for overcaller's minor

teachingLabel.name: 2H — bypass (6+ hearts)
teachingLabel.summary: Bypass the relay with 6+ hearts, placing the contract directly

teachingLabel.name: 2S — bypass (6+ spades)
teachingLabel.summary: Bypass the relay with 6+ spades, placing the contract directly

teachingLabel.name: 2D — relay (ask for higher suit)
teachingLabel.summary: Ask overcaller to reveal which higher suit they hold alongside clubs

teachingLabel.name: Pass — accept spades
teachingLabel.summary: Accept spades as trumps with 3+ card support for overcaller's suit

teachingLabel.name: 3C — escape (6+ clubs)
teachingLabel.summary: Escape to clubs with a 6+ card suit, rejecting spades as trumps

teachingLabel.name: 3D — escape (6+ diamonds)
teachingLabel.summary: Escape to diamonds with a 6+ card suit, rejecting spades as trumps

teachingLabel.name: 3H — escape (6+ hearts)
teachingLabel.summary: Escape to hearts with a 6+ card suit, rejecting spades as trumps

teachingLabel.name: Pass (no alternative)
teachingLabel.summary: Accept spades by default when no long suit provides a better escape

teachingLabel.name: 2D — bypass (6+ diamonds)
teachingLabel.summary: Bypass the relay with 6+ diamonds, placing the contract directly

teachingLabel.name: 2H — bypass (6+ hearts)
teachingLabel.summary: Bypass the relay with 6+ hearts, placing the contract directly

teachingLabel.name: 2S — bypass (6+ spades)
teachingLabel.summary: Bypass the relay with 6+ spades, placing the contract directly

teachingLabel.name: 2C — forced relay after double
teachingLabel.summary: Relay to 2C so overcaller can reveal their single long suit

teachingLabel.name: Pass — clubs
teachingLabel.summary: Reveal clubs as the long suit by passing the 2C relay

teachingLabel.name: 2D — diamonds
teachingLabel.summary: Reveal diamonds as the long suit by correcting from the 2C relay

teachingLabel.name: 2H — hearts
teachingLabel.summary: Reveal hearts as the long suit by correcting from the 2C relay

teachingLabel.name: Pass — diamonds (from 2C+higher)
teachingLabel.summary: Reveal diamonds as the higher suit by passing the 2D relay

teachingLabel.name: 2H — hearts (from 2C+higher)
teachingLabel.summary: Reveal hearts as the higher suit alongside clubs

teachingLabel.name: 2S — spades (from 2C+higher)
teachingLabel.summary: Reveal spades as the higher suit alongside clubs

teachingLabel.name: Pass — hearts (from 2D+major)
teachingLabel.summary: Reveal hearts as the major by passing the 2H relay

teachingLabel.name: 2S — spades (from 2D+major)
teachingLabel.summary: Reveal spades as the major alongside diamonds

definitions.description: H5+S4+ or S5+H4+ — both majors for 2H bid
D4+ and (H4+ or S4+) — diamonds + a major for 2D bid
C4+ and (D4+ or H4+ or S4+) — clubs + higher suit for 2C bid
One suit 6+, no other suit 4+, longest suit is not spades — for double
With clubs as anchor, higher suit is diamonds
With clubs as anchor, higher suit is hearts
With clubs as anchor, higher suit is spades
With diamonds as anchor, the major is hearts
With diamonds as anchor, the major is spades
3+ hearts, or equal length in both majors (2-2)
6+ in clubs or diamonds (for minor escape)
